reforms.description: The government initiated a Plan for Growth that includes measures to encourage investment and exports, aiming to make the UK the best place in Europe to start and grow a business, and to create a more educated workforce.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a competitive and innovative environment, the plan aims to enhance entrepreneurship and job creation, which are crucial for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Significant progress in implementation has already been made in most of these areas.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Plan for Growth represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing the business environment and supporting innovation, which is critical for long-term economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The government initiated a Plan for Growth (2011) that sets out an ambitious set of measures to encourage investment and exports.

reforms.description: The government has considered giving the Green Investment Bank powers to borrow from 2015/16 to enhance investment in green infrastructure, which includes energy, water, transport, and waste.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enabling the Green Investment Bank to borrow, the government aims to increase funding for green infrastructure projects, which can stimulate economic growth and innovation in sustainable technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has considered giving the Green Investment Bank powers to borrow.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance funding for green infrastructure, which is essential for sustainable economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has considered giving the Green Investment Bank powers to borrow from 2015/16.

reforms.description: The UK government aims to enhance the potential of the economy through a Plan for Growth, which includes measures to support innovation and the development of green technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting innovation and green technologies can lead to increased productivity and sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government set a Plan for Growth to enhance the economy's potential.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Plan for Growth represents a significant structural change aimed at improving the overall economic framework and productivity through innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A Plan for Growth set by the government aims at enhancing the potential of the economy and rebalancing towards exports and investment.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to improve R&D intensity by better rewarding innovation through the tax system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing tax incentives for R&D can stimulate private sector investment in innovation, leading to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to address low R&D intensity by better rewarding innovation through the tax system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major shift in the approach to incentivizing R&D, which is crucial for enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The low R&D intensity compared to other OECD countries could be addressed by better rewarding innovation through the tax system.

reforms.description: The UK Government initiated the Growth Accelerator programme to support SMEs with high growth potential by facilitating access to skills development, mentoring, and coaching.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the capabilities of high-potential SMEs, the programme aims to stimulate innovation and productivity growth in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been launched to support SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to enhance support for SMEs, which are crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The UK Government has recently introduced the Growth Accelerator programme to support SMEs with high growth potential.

reforms.description: The National Loan Guarantee Scheme was launched to help smaller businesses access affordable finance by providing government guarantees on unsecured borrowing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing borrowing costs for SMEs, the scheme aims to enhance their financial capacity to invest in innovation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has provided over £2.5 billion in loans to businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scheme is a significant initiative to improve access to finance for SMEs, which is critical for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The National Loan Guarantee Scheme (NLGS) was launched on 20 March 2012 to help smaller businesses to access affordable finance.

reforms.description: The Coalition Government increased support for R&D by SMEs through both tax and spending measures, raising the rate of relief under the SME R&D tax credit scheme to 225% of qualifying R&D costs from April 2012.
reforms.package_name: 2011 Innovation and Research Strategy for Growth
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in tax relief incentivizes SMEs to invest more in R&D, which can lead to greater innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The rate of relief under the SME R&D tax credit scheme increased as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the support for R&D, enhancing the competitiveness of SMEs and potentially leading to substantial increases in R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: From 1 April 2012, the rate of relief under the SME R&D tax credit scheme increases to 225% of qualifying R&D costs, one of the most competitive rates in the world.

reforms.description: The UK Research Investment Partnership Fund was launched with £100 million of public money to support new university research infrastructure built in partnership with business and charities.
reforms.package_name: 2011 Innovation and Research Strategy for Growth
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This fund aims to enhance research capabilities in universities, fostering collaboration with the private sector and driving innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund was launched with the specified amount of public money.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in research infrastructure, promoting long-term strategic partnerships between universities and the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: A UK Research Investment Partnership Fund was launched with £100 million of public money to support new university research infrastructure built in partnership with business and charities.

reforms.description: The government announced an injection of £300 million to provide up to an additional 15,000 affordable homes through loan guarantees and capital funding, aiming to address the gap in social housing supply.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to stimulate construction activity and improve housing availability, which can enhance overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced that it will inject £300 million to provide up to an additional 15,000 affordable homes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding represents a significant effort to address housing shortages, which is a critical issue for economic growth and social stability.
reforms.source_quote: In September 2012, the government announced that it will inject £300 million to provide up to an additional 15,000 affordable homes through the use of loan guarantees, asset management flexibilities and capital funding.

reforms.description: The New Homes Bonus was introduced to create financial incentives for local authorities to promote housing growth by matching funding from council tax on expanded local housing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing local authorities to increase housing supply, this initiative aims to alleviate housing shortages and stimulate local economies.
reforms.status_evidence: The New Homes Bonus is designed to create incentives for local authorities to promote growth.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant policy shift aimed at addressing local opposition to housing development and encouraging investment in housing.
reforms.source_quote: The New Homes Bonus is designed to create incentives for local authorities to promote growth.

reforms.description: The government plans to reform tax rules to encourage R&D, aiming to enhance firm growth by reviewing fiscal rules that may hinder innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging R&D through tax reforms can stimulate innovation and firm growth, leading to increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The text recommends reforms to tax rules to encourage R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the tax framework affecting R&D, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: Reform some tax rules to encourage R&D.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to reform some tax rules to encourage R&D, aiming to review fiscal rules that may hinder firm growth, such as preferential tax treatment for small firms and debt finance relative to equity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging R&D through tax reforms can stimulate innovation and firm growth, leading to increased productivity and economic expansion.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to reform tax rules to encourage R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the fiscal framework affecting R&D investment, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Reform some tax rules to encourage R&D. Review fiscal rules which may hamper firm growth, such as preferential tax treatment for small firms and debt finance relative to equity.

reforms.description: The government will revise the current R&D subsidy system to increase its effectiveness as an incentive, aiming to reward social returns to R&D that exceed private returns.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the effectiveness of R&D subsidies, the reform is expected to stimulate innovation and increase overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government will change support for R&D through revising the current R&D subsidy system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the R&D subsidy system, which is crucial for enhancing innovation incentives and driving economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government will change support for R&D through revising the current R&D subsidy system to increase its effectiveness as an incentive.

reforms.description: From April 2013, the government will introduce a 'Patent Box' to provide beneficial tax treatment for profits related to patents, aimed at driving growth and investment in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The 'Patent Box' is designed to incentivize innovation by providing tax benefits, which can stimulate investment in research and development activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The 'Patent Box' is intended to create social returns by supporting innovation more broadly.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the 'Patent Box' represents a major policy shift aimed at enhancing the attractiveness of the UK for innovative firms and fostering economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: From April 2013, the government will also be introducing a 'Patent Box' to drive growth and investment in innovation.
